name: Jordan Kim
backstory: Jordan saw a YouTube video about Obsidian PKM and got inspired to build a 'second brain' for college. They downloaded Obsidian, watched 4 hours of tutorials, created 200 notes that all link to each other, and now have a vault that feels more overwhelming than not having one. They want to feel in control of their coursework, not like they're building a hobby project instead of studying. They're slightly embarrassed they spent so much time on the system without it helping them study.
emotional_triggers: System complexity for its own sake — they're starting to resent Obsidian
Feeling like they're behind — both on their vault and on actual coursework
Advice that assumes they have hours to spend tweaking — they need fast wins
existing_strengths: High curiosity — genuinely interested in learning how to learn
Already has an Obsidian vault with real content, just poorly organized
Quick to adopt new habits if the benefit is immediately visible
goals: Build a note system that actually helps during exams
Stop spending more time on the system than on studying
Understand Dataview enough to make useful study dashboards
success_looks_like: Jordan uses their vault to prepare for one real exam and feels like it helped.
failure_looks_like: Jordan gets taught every Dataview operator before building a single useful query.
